rules.title: Accessibility Features Backdoor
rules.description: Detects replacement of accessibility programs (sethc, utilman, osk) with cmd.exe for pre-login access.

rules.title: Account Access Removal
rules.description: Detects deletion or disabling of user accounts which may indicate an attempt to lock out legitimate users.

rules.title: Active Setup Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of Active Setup entries in the registry for per-user persistence.

rules.title: AD Enumeration via ADFind
rules.description: Detects usage of AdFind.exe for Active Directory enumeration and reconnaissance.

rules.title: ADCS ESC1: Subject Alternative Name Abuse via Certipy/Certify
rules.description: Detects ADCS ESC1 abuse where an attacker requests a certificate from a vulnerable template that allows enrollee-supplied subject and uses an alternate UPN (SAN) to impersonate a privileged account. Certipy and Certify expose this via the 'req' action with -upn/-template/-altname. In GOAD this is run against the ESC1 template on the CA to impersonate a Domain Admin.

rules.title: ADCS ESC4: Certificate Template ACL Modification via Certipy
rules.description: Detects ESC4 abuse where an attacker with write access to a certificate template reconfigures it to become ESC1-vulnerable (enrollee-supplied SAN, client-auth EKU). Certipy 'template' with -write/-save performs this template takeover before requesting an impersonation certificate.

rules.title: ADCS ESC6: EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 Flag Enabled via Certutil
rules.description: Detects enabling of the EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 policy flag on an Enterprise CA (ESC6). When set, any requester can specify an arbitrary SAN on any certificate request, allowing domain-wide impersonation. Set via 'certutil -setreg policy\EditFlags'. GOAD demonstrates this on the SRV03 CA.

rules.title: ADCS ESC8: NTLM Relay to Certificate Web Enrollment
rules.description: Detects ESC8 attacks relaying coerced NTLM authentication to the ADCS HTTP web-enrollment endpoint (certsrv) to obtain a certificate for a DC or privileged account. Indicators include Certipy 'relay' mode and ntlmrelayx targeting /certsrv/certfnsh.asp. In GOAD this is chained with PetitPotam.

rules.title: AdminSDHolder ACL Modification for Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of the AdminSDHolder container ACL (or dsacls against it) to plant a backdoor that SDProp re-applies to all protected groups every 60 minutes, granting durable privileged access to Domain Admins and similar.

rules.title: Alternate Data Stream Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of payloads hidden in NTFS Alternate Data Streams.

rules.title: AlwaysInstallElevated Exploitation
rules.description: Detects exploitation of AlwaysInstallElevated policy for privilege escalation via MSI.

rules.title: AMSI Bypass via PowerShell Reflection
rules.description: Detects PowerShell reflection techniques used to bypass AMSI by patching the AmsiScanBuffer function.

rules.title: API Endpoint Unauthorized Access Attempts
rules.description: Detects unauthorized access attempts to API endpoints (401/403 responses). May indicate API key brute force, token manipulation, or broken access control. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: APM: SQL Injection Attack in Request
rules.description: Detects SQL injection payloads inside APM-instrumented application requests. Matches UNION SELECT, OR 1=1, time-based blind (SLEEP), boolean-based, error-based (EXTRACTVALUE), and stacked-query patterns appearing in Request URL.

rules.title: AppInit DLLs Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of AppInit_DLLs registry value to inject DLLs into every process that loads User32.dll.

rules.title: Application Shimming for Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of application compatibility shims using sdbinst.exe.

rules.title: AppLocker Policy Bypass via MSHTML
rules.description: Detects MSHTA or similar MSHTML-based execution paths used to bypass AppLocker policies.

rules.title: AS-REP Roasting via Rubeus
rules.description: Detects AS-REP Roasting attempts using Rubeus or similar tools targeting accounts without Kerberos pre-authentication.

rules.title: BITS Job Persistence
rules.description: Detects BITS jobs created for persistent execution or file download.

rules.title: BloodHound AD Enumeration
rules.description: Detects execution of BloodHound or SharpHound for Active Directory reconnaissance.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Browser Spawning Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects Internet Explorer spawning command interpreters or script hosts. After exploiting CVE-2020-1380 or CVE-2021-26411, BLUELIGHT uses iexplore.exe to spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or wscript.exe for second-stage payload execution.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Browser Credential Memory Access
rules.description: Detects non-browser processes accessing browser process memory with full access rights. BLUELIGHT reads browser memory (GrantedAccess 0x1fffff = PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) to extract stored credentials, session tokens, and cookies from Chrome, Edge, Firefox.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Internet Explorer Drive-by Compromise
rules.description: Detects Internet Explorer making network connections to non-Microsoft domains. BLUELIGHT (S0657) uses browser exploits (CVE-2020-1380, CVE-2021-26411) for initial compromise via watering hole attacks. APT37/InkySquid abuses IE vulnerabilities to deliver the BLUELIGHT payload through malicious JavaScript on compromised sites.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: File Discovery from Browser Process
rules.description: Detects recursive directory enumeration spawned by browser or script host processes. BLUELIGHT performs file and directory discovery to locate sensitive documents, credentials, and system files for later collection and exfiltration.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: C2 via Microsoft Graph API
rules.description: Detects non-standard processes connecting to Microsoft Graph API endpoints. BLUELIGHT uses Microsoft Graph API and OneDrive for command-and-control, blending with legitimate Microsoft traffic to evade network detection.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Executable Download via Graph API
rules.description: Detects executable or DLL files dropped in temporary directories by processes that also connect to Microsoft Graph API. BLUELIGHT downloads additional tools from OneDrive via Graph API and stages them in Temp or AppData for execution.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Obfuscated Script Execution
rules.description: Detects obfuscation techniques in command lines indicative of BLUELIGHT payload delivery. The RAT uses XOR encryption (key 0xCF), Base64 encoding, and SVG-embedded JavaScript for defense evasion during initial exploitation.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Data Exfiltration via OneDrive/Graph API
rules.description: Detects large data uploads to Microsoft Graph API from non-OneDrive processes. BLUELIGHT exfiltrates collected screenshots, credentials, and system data by uploading to OneDrive via Graph API, blending with legitimate cloud storage traffic.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Registry Enumeration of Security Products
rules.description: Detects registry access targeting security product and installed software keys. BLUELIGHT enumerates SecurityCenter2, Uninstall keys, and AV service registrations to identify defensive tools before attempting further payload delivery.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Periodic Screen Capture
rules.description: Detects rapid JPEG file creation by non-image processes. BLUELIGHT captures screenshots at regular intervals and saves them as .jpg files before staging for exfiltration via OneDrive. High frequency (>3 per minute) indicates automated capture.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: WMI System Enumeration from Browser Child
rules.description: Detects WMI-based system enumeration spawned by browser processes. After initial exploitation, BLUELIGHT queries Win32_ComputerSystem, Win32_OperatingSystem, and Win32_Processor to profile the target system before establishing full C2.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Chrome/Edge Cookie Theft (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
rules.description: Detects BLUELIGHT browser cookie theft targeting Google authentication. The malware extracts SID, OSID, __Secure-3PSID cookies from Chrome/Edge to hijack Gmail and Google Account sessions. Enables IMAP access on victim Gmail accounts for persistent email exfiltration. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $chrome strings.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Google App C2 Communication (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
rules.description: Detects BLUELIGHT C2 communication via Google services. The variant uses custom Google App authentication with spoofed Firefox User-Agent and Korean language Accept-Language headers (ko-KR). Accesses Gmail and Google Account settings to enable IMAP for email exfiltration. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $chrome strings.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Keylogger Component (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
rules.description: Detects BLUELIGHT keylogger component strings. The malware logs keystrokes with modifier prefixes (SHIFT+, CTRL+, CAPSLOCK+, ALT+) and special key markers ([TAB], [RETURN], [ESC], [ARROW *], [DEL], [WIN]). The keylogger output is staged for exfiltration via OneDrive. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $keylogger_component strings (27 of 29 required).

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA PDB Path Indicators (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight)
rules.description: Detects BLUELIGHT malware PDB path strings in process memory, file content, or Sysmon ImageLoaded events. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight (id: 3ec2d44c). The PDB paths 'Development\BACKDOOR\ncov\' and 'Release\bluelight.pdb' are unique to BLUELIGHT compilation artifacts.

rules.title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA System Reconnaissance JSON (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight)
rules.description: Detects BLUELIGHT system reconnaissance data collection pattern. The malware collects UserName, ComName, OS, OnlineIP, LocalIP, Time, Compiled, Process Level, AntiVirus, and VM detection results in a specific JSON format and exfiltrates via OneDrive. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight $msg strings.

rules.title: Boot Configuration Change for Persistence
rules.description: Detects BCDEdit commands that modify boot configuration for safe mode persistence.

rules.title: Browser Credential Store Access
rules.description: Detects processes accessing browser credential stores (Chrome Login Data, Firefox logins.json).

rules.title: Brute Force: Failed Logon Spike per Account
rules.description: Detects 3+ failed logon attempts (4625) for a single account — indicates password spraying, brute force, or credential stuffing.

rules.title: Caldera - Credential Search in Config and Environment Files (Windows)
rules.description: Detects broad-scope search for credentials stored in configuration files, scripts, and environment files. Caldera T1552 emulation runs findstr with password-related keywords across common file types and user profile directories, extending beyond the specific filename-based search in credential_file_discovery.yaml.

rules.title: Caldera - Credential Search in Files via grep (Linux)
rules.description: Detects use of grep or find to search for credentials stored in configuration and history files. Caldera T1552.001 emulation runs grep -r with keywords like password, passwd, secret, token, and apikey across common configuration paths. Complements the SSRF/metadata credential rules with file-system-based grep detection.

rules.title: Caldera - Data Collection from Local System via findstr and PowerShell (Windows)
rules.description: Detects collection of sensitive documents and data files from local directories. Caldera T1005 emulation searches for and copies high-value files (documents, spreadsheets, databases, source code) from user profiles and common data paths to a staging location. Complements credential_file_discovery with broader document collection.

rules.title: Caldera - Data Collection from Network Shared Drive (Linux)
rules.description: Detects collection of data from mounted network shares on Linux hosts. Caldera T1039 emulation accesses SMB/CIFS and NFS mounts, lists their contents, and copies files to a staging location. Patterns include smbclient interactive commands, direct reads from /mnt share paths, and cifs-utils access.

rules.title: Caldera - Data Collection from Network Shared Drive (Windows)
rules.description: Detects bulk file collection from UNC/admin share paths on Windows systems. Caldera T1039 emulation copies or moves files from mapped network drives and UNC paths to a local staging directory before exfiltration. Patterns include xcopy, robocopy, and PowerShell Copy-Item with remote share source paths.

rules.title: Caldera - Document and Data File Collection from Linux Host
rules.description: Detects collection of document and data files from user home directories and common data paths. Caldera T1005 emulation searches for and copies high-value files (office docs, PDFs, databases, source code archives) using find and cp. Complements linux_sensitive_data_access.yaml which focuses on SSH keys and cloud credential files.

rules.title: Caldera - Exfiltration of Staged Data over C2 Channel (Linux)
rules.description: Detects Linux-side exfiltration of collected data over the C2 channel using HTTP POST requests. Caldera T1041 emulation transmits staged files from /tmp or /dev/shm back to the C2 server using curl or wget with POST/PUT methods and multipart form uploads, distinct from the alternative-protocol T1048 pattern.

rules.title: Caldera - Exfiltration over C2 Channel via PowerShell (Windows)
rules.description: Detects PowerShell-based HTTP POST exfiltration used by Caldera T1041 emulation. After staging data locally, the operation transmits collected files using Invoke-WebRequest or Invoke-RestMethod with -Method POST and -Body/-InFile parameters to send data back over the established C2 channel.

rules.title: Caldera - Exfiltration to Web Service from Windows Endpoint
rules.description: Detects uploads of collected data to public web services from Windows hosts. Caldera T1567 emulation uses PowerShell or curl to push staged files to paste sites, anonymous file-share services, or cloud storage APIs. This complements the OneDrive-specific BLUELIGHT APT rule with a general cross-service detection.

rules.title: Caldera - Exfiltration to Web Service via curl (Linux)
rules.description: Detects Linux-side data upload to public web services used as exfiltration channels. Caldera T1567 emulation uploads staged files to paste sites, cloud storage APIs (S3, GCS), or anonymous file-sharing services using curl. Covers the endpoint-level activity that OCI network monitoring may miss.

rules.title: Caldera - Lateral Tool Transfer via scp/rsync (Linux)
rules.description: Detects transfer of attacker tools or payloads to other hosts via scp, rsync, or curl. Caldera T1570 emulation copies implants from the initial foothold to lateral targets over SSH (scp) or HTTP (curl download-and-execute). Detection focuses on transfers from /tmp or /dev/shm to remote hosts.

rules.title: Caldera - Local Data Staging in Writable Directories (Linux)
rules.description: Detects data staging in world-writable directories prior to exfiltration. Caldera T1074 emulation copies or moves sensitive files into /tmp, /dev/shm, or /var/tmp before transmitting them. Detection is based on bulk copy or move operations targeting these staging paths from sensitive source directories.

rules.title: Caldera - LSASS Memory Dump via ProcDump or Task Manager (Caldera Emulation)
rules.description: Detects LSASS credential dumping specifically as emulated by Caldera T1003.001 operations. Caldera's credential-access adversary profile leverages ProcDump, Task Manager, or comsvcs.dll MiniDump to extract LSASS memory. This rule covers the ProcDump command-line pattern and the Task Manager dump output file name, complementing the existing lsass_memory_dump_via_comsvcs_dll.yaml.

rules.title: Caldera - Network Configuration Recon via ipconfig and route (Windows)
rules.description: Detects ipconfig and route commands used by Caldera T1016 discovery emulation on Windows. The operation chains ipconfig /all, route print, and arp -a to enumerate all interfaces, gateway routes, and ARP cache for network mapping. This process_creation level rule complements the discovery/caldera_t1016 rule with specific binary-level matching.

rules.title: Caldera - Remote Data Staging via SMB Share Write
rules.description: Detects staging of collected data on remote SMB shares before exfiltration. Caldera T1074.002 emulation copies files to a UNC share path accessible from the attacker system, consolidating data from multiple compromised hosts. Patterns include net use with copy/xcopy writing to remote UNC destinations.

rules.title: Caldera - SMB Lateral Movement via Impacket from Linux
rules.description: Detects use of Impacket tools (psexec.py, smbexec.py, wmiexec.py) from Linux hosts for lateral movement to Windows targets. Caldera T1021.002 emulation leverages Impacket to authenticate over SMB and execute commands on Windows systems within the GOAD AD lab network.

rules.title: Caldera - System Information Discovery via Native Linux Commands
rules.description: Detects use of native Linux commands to profile system hardware, OS version, and kernel details. Caldera T1082 emulation runs uname, hostnamectl, lscpu, dmidecode, and /etc/os-release reads to fingerprint the host before exploitation. This rule complements linpeas/linenum detection by covering non-script-based recon.

rules.title: Caldera - System Information Discovery via systeminfo and WMI (Windows)
rules.description: Detects non-APT system reconnaissance commands used by Caldera T1082 emulation. The operation runs systeminfo.exe, wmic commands, and PowerShell Get-ComputerInfo to enumerate OS version, hotfix history, BIOS details, and hardware specs. Complements the BLUELIGHT browser-child WMI rule with general emulation patterns.

rules.title: Caldera - System Network Configuration Discovery (Linux)
rules.description: Detects Linux commands used to enumerate network interface configuration. Caldera T1016 emulation runs ip addr/route/link, ifconfig, route -n, and arp to map network topology and identify active interfaces after initial access.

rules.title: Caldera - System Network Configuration Discovery (Windows)
rules.description: Detects network configuration enumeration on Windows systems as emulated by Caldera T1016 operations. Adversaries run ipconfig, arp, nslookup, and PowerShell network cmdlets to identify interfaces, DNS settings, and proxies.

rules.title: Caldera - System Network Connections Discovery (Linux)
rules.description: Detects enumeration of active network connections on Linux systems. Caldera T1049 emulation uses netstat, ss, and /proc/net file reads to list established connections, listening ports, and socket state for network mapping.

rules.title: Caldera - System Network Connections Discovery (Windows)
rules.description: Detects enumeration of active network connections on Windows systems. Caldera T1049 emulation runs netstat.exe and PowerShell Get-NetTCPConnection to list established connections, listening ports, and routing tables for network topology mapping after initial access.

rules.title: Caldera - System Owner and Identity Discovery via id/whoami (Linux)
rules.description: Detects identity enumeration commands used by Caldera T1033 emulation on Linux. Adversaries run id, whoami, logname, and finger immediately after access to determine their effective privilege level and active username. This rule covers the quick identity check commands not included in the existing T1033 rule.

rules.title: Caldera - System Owner and User Discovery (Windows)
rules.description: Detects commands used to discover the current system owner and logged-in users. Caldera T1033 emulation runs whoami, query user, and PowerShell Get-LocalUser to determine privilege context, active sessions, and user account details immediately after gaining access to a Windows endpoint.

rules.title: Caldera - System Owner Discovery via whoami and query Commands (Windows)
rules.description: Process-creation level detection of whoami.exe, query.exe, and net user for identity and session enumeration. Caldera T1033 emulation immediately runs whoami /all to determine privilege level and group memberships, and query user to list active sessions. This binary-level rule complements the discovery-category rule that uses CommandLine matching.

rules.title: Caldera - WinRM Lateral Movement via Evil-WinRM from Linux
rules.description: Detects use of Evil-WinRM or Python WinRM clients from Linux hosts for lateral movement to Windows targets. Caldera T1021.006 emulation connects to WinRM (port 5985/5986) on Windows machines in the GOAD network using stolen credentials or pass-the-hash to execute commands remotely.

rules.title: Caldera - WMIC OS and Hardware Enumeration (Windows)
rules.description: Detects WMIC-based system profiling commands used by Caldera T1082 discovery operations. The Caldera discovery adversary profile queries Win32_OperatingSystem, Win32_ComputerSystem, Win32_Processor, and installed software to build a comprehensive host profile. This process_creation level rule focuses on wmic.exe command-line patterns distinct from the PowerShell Get-WmiObject variant.

rules.title: Caldera/OpenAEV - Exfiltration via OCI Object Storage Bulk Upload with New Bucket
rules.description: Detects creation of a new OCI Object Storage bucket immediately followed by object uploads, a pattern consistent with OpenAEV/Caldera T1567 emulation using cloud storage as an exfiltration channel. Distinguishes from the pre-auth request rule by focusing on the bucket creation + upload sequence.

rules.title: Caldera/OpenAEV - Transfer Data to Cloud Account via OCI Object Storage
rules.description: Detects bulk data upload to OCI Object Storage as part of exfiltration to a cloud account. OpenAEV/Caldera T1537 emulation copies staged files to an OCI bucket that may be attacker-controlled or pre-authenticated for external access. Triggers on PutObject API calls following unusual IAM session activity.

rules.title: Certifried (CVE-2022-26923): Machine Account dNSHostName Abuse
rules.description: Detects Certifried, where an attacker-controlled computer account has its dNSHostName set to match a DC and then requests a machine certificate to impersonate the DC. Indicators: Certipy 'req'/'account' chained with a dnshostname write. GOAD lists Certifried alongside the ESC paths.

rules.title: ClickFix Fake CAPTCHA PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects browser-launched PowerShell patterns associated with ClickFix fake CAPTCHA social engineering, including clipboard instructions, hidden execution, and download cradles.

references.name: Microsoft Security Blog: Think before you ClickFix

rules.title: Clipboard Data Collection
rules.description: Detects PowerShell or command-line access to clipboard data for credential or data theft.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: Findings by Host
rules.description: Ranks Cloud Guard Instance Security findings by host and OCI compute instance.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: Findings by Pack Query
rules.description: Shows which OSQuery pack queries are producing the most findings.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: High Severity Pivots
rules.description: High-severity workload findings with MITRE, host, process, and network pivots.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: Instance to Query Link View
rules.description: Links OCI instances, hosts, OSQuery pack queries, and finding names for investigation.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: Pack Coverage
rules.description: Counts Cloud Guard Instance Security and OSQuery result events by pack, severity, and status.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Instance Security: Raw Result Detail
rules.description: Raw Cloud Guard Instance Security and OSQuery result detail for analyst review.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Audit Log Retention
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Audit_Log_Retention.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Bucket Public Read
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Bucket_Public_Read.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Bucket Public Write
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Bucket_Public_Write.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Group Has Too Many Admins
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Group_Has_Too_Many_Admins.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: IAM User API Key Old
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: IAM_User_API_Key_Old.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: IAM User Console Password Old
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: IAM_User_Console_Password_Old.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Instance Principals Enabled
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Instance_Principals_Enabled.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: INSTANCE PUBLIC IP
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: INSTANCE_PUBLIC_IP.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Policy Too Permissive
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Policy_Too_Permissive.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: VCN Flow Log Disabled
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: VCN_Flow_Log_Disabled.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: VCN Security List Port RDP
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: VCN_Security_List_Port_RDP.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: VCN Security List Port SSH
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: VCN_Security_List_Port_SSH.

rules.title: CMD: Suspicious Command Execution (Real Windows Security Events)
rules.description: Detects suspicious cmd.exe commands in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation logs. Covers reconnaissance, persistence, lateral movement, and defense evasion commands commonly used by attackers after initial compromise.

rules.title: CMSTP UAC Bypass
rules.description: Detects usage of CMSTP.exe to bypass UAC and execute arbitrary commands through .inf files.

rules.title: Coercer Multi-Protocol Authentication Coercion
rules.description: Detects the Coercer tool, which automates authentication coercion across multiple RPC interfaces (MS-EFSRPC, MS-RPRN, MS-DFSNM, MS-FSRVP, MS-EVEN) to force a target to authenticate to an attacker host for NTLM relay.

rules.title: COM Object Hijacking via Registry
rules.description: Detects modification of COM object registry entries to hijack execution flow.

rules.title: Compiled HTML File Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of compiled HTML (.chm) files via hh.exe, which can execute embedded scripts.

rules.title: Computer Account Creation for Delegation Abuse (MachineAccountQuota)
rules.description: Detects creation of a new computer account by an unprivileged user abusing the default MachineAccountQuota (10). Attacker-created machine accounts are the pivot for RBCD takeovers and Certifried (CVE-2022-26923). Tools include impacket addcomputer.py and PowerMad New-MachineAccount.

rules.title: Constrained Delegation S4U2Proxy Abuse via Rubeus
rules.description: Detects abuse of Kerberos constrained delegation (S4U2self + S4U2proxy) via Rubeus 's4u' to impersonate a privileged user to a target service (often CIFS/HOST/LDAP on a DC). GOAD demonstrates this from accounts trusted for delegation.

rules.title: Control Panel Item Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of .cpl files which can be used to execute arbitrary DLLs.

rules.title: Credential Access via Certutil Certificate Export
rules.description: Detects usage of certutil.exe to export certificates which may contain private keys for authentication.

rules.title: Credential Dumping via Comsvcs with Rundll32
rules.description: Detects usage of rundll32 with comsvcs.dll to dump process memory, commonly targeting LSASS.

rules.title: Credential Dumping via Windows Task Manager
rules.description: Detects creation of LSASS dump files by Task Manager, which can be used to extract credentials.

rules.title: Credential File Discovery
rules.description: Detects searches for files commonly containing credentials such as passwords.txt, credentials.xml, or web.config.

rules.title: Credential Manager: High-Frequency Credential Read
rules.description: Detects Event 5379 credential read operations at high frequency — indicates lazagne, seatbelt, or mimikatz vault::cred dumping stored credentials.

rules.title: Cryptominer Deployment Indicators
rules.description: Detects indicators of cryptocurrency mining software deployment on Windows systems.

rules.title: Data Compression for Exfiltration via 7zip
rules.description: Detects data compression using 7-Zip or WinRAR with password protection, often used before exfiltration.

rules.title: DCOM Lateral Movement via MMC20
rules.description: Detects lateral movement using DCOM MMC20.Application object to execute commands remotely.

rules.title: DCShadow Rogue Domain Controller Registration
rules.description: Detects DCShadow, where mimikatz registers a rogue Domain Controller (lsadump::dcshadow) to push malicious directory replication (e.g. SID History or AdminSDHolder changes) while bypassing normal change logging.

rules.title: DCSync Attack via Replication Request
rules.description: Detects potential DCSync attacks by looking for mimikatz-style commands requesting directory replication.

rules.title: DCSync: Directory Replication from Non-Domain Controller
rules.description: Detects directory replication requests (Event 4662) with DS-Replication-Get-Changes GUIDs from non-machine accounts — classic DCSync attack via mimikatz lsadump::dcsync.

rules.title: DCSync Enablement: Replication Rights Granted via Directory Change (5136)
rules.description: Detects the persistence/setup step for DCSync, where DS-Replication-Get-Changes and -Get-Changes-All rights are granted to a non-DC principal by modifying the domain object ACL (Event 5136 carrying the replication control-access-right GUIDs in its properties). Complements the existing 4662 DCSync detection by catching the rights grant itself.

rules.title: Defacement via Desktop Wallpaper Change
rules.description: Detects commands changing the desktop wallpaper, which may indicate ransomware defacement.

rules.title: Default File Association Hijack
rules.description: Detects modification of default file associations for persistence.

rules.title: DFSCoerce MS-DFSNM Authentication Coercion
rules.description: Detects DFSCoerce, which abuses the MS-DFSNM (NetrDfsRemoveStdRoot / NetrDfsAddStdRoot) interface to coerce a Domain Controller into authenticating to an attacker-controlled host for NTLM relay.

rules.title: Disable Windows Firewall via Netsh
rules.description: Detects commands to disable the Windows firewall using netsh, enabling network-based attacks.

rules.title: Disk Wipe via Format Command
rules.description: Detects format command execution that could indicate destructive disk wiping.

rules.title: DLL Execution via Rundll32 from User Path
rules.description: Detects rundll32.exe loading DLLs from user-writable directories.

rules.title: DLL Hijacking via Service Registry Permission
rules.description: Detects modification of service registry entries to point to attacker-controlled DLLs.

rules.title: DLL Side-Loading from Suspicious Directory
rules.description: Detects execution of legitimate signed binaries from temp or user-writable directories, suggesting DLL side-loading.

rules.title: Domain Admins Group Enumeration via net group
rules.description: Detects enumeration of high-value domain groups (Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins) using 'net group /domain'. Standard early-stage AD recon to map privileged accounts before targeting them.

rules.title: Domain Trust Discovery via Nltest
rules.description: Detects domain trust enumeration using nltest.exe.

rules.title: DPAPI Master Key Extraction
rules.description: Detects extraction of DPAPI master keys which protect user credentials, browser passwords, and other secrets.

rules.title: DSRM Admin Logon Behavior Enabled for Persistence
rules.description: Detects enabling of DsrmAdminLogonBehavior (registry value 2) on a Domain Controller, which lets the Directory Services Restore Mode local admin authenticate over the network — a stealthy DC backdoor once the DSRM hash is synced (e.g. via mimikatz lsadump::dcsync or set-ntlm).

rules.title: Email Collection via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands accessing Outlook COM objects or .pst files for email collection.

rules.title: ETW Provider Disabled
rules.description: Detects disabling of ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) providers, which can blind security monitoring.

rules.title: Executable Written to Remote Admin Share (Lateral Tool Transfer)
rules.description: Detects staging of a payload to a remote administrative share (ADMIN$, C$, IPC$) prior to service- or task-based execution. Impacket smbexec/psexec and manual 'copy \\host\C$' transfers leave this artifact during lateral movement across the GOAD estate.

rules.title: File and Directory Discovery via dir
rules.description: Detects recursive directory listings often used during post-exploitation to find sensitive files.

rules.title: File Deletion of Security Tools
rules.description: Detects deletion of security-related files and configurations that could impair defenses.

rules.title: Finger.exe Abuse for File Download
rules.description: Detects abuse of finger.exe to download files or exfiltrate data via the finger protocol.

rules.title: Forced Authentication via PetitPotam
rules.description: Detects PetitPotam and related NTLM relay tools that force authentication to attacker-controlled servers.

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: Adversarial / Obfuscated Guardrail Evasion (AML.T0043)
rules.description: Detects crafted adversarial payloads aimed at evading GenAI gateway guardrails (zero-width/bidi unicode smuggling, base64 payloads, chat-template control tokens, char-code construction). Fires on the evasion/obfuscation guardrail category, ranking caller identity, API key, and source IP. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0043 (Craft Adversarial Data) used for evasion.

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: Denial of ML Service / Inference Flood (AML.T0029 / AML.T0046)
rules.description: Surfaces request floods and chaff/spam against the GenAI gateway, the signature of Denial of ML Service and resource exhaustion. Aggregates throttled/rate-limited inference traffic (429 or THROTTLE verdict) by source IP and API key. Triage threshold: investigate sources with throttle counts far above baseline. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0029 (Denial of ML Service) and AML.T0046 (Spamming ML System with Chaff Data).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: Bulk Inference Exfiltration / Model Extraction (AML.T0024)
rules.description: Surfaces callers driving high inference volume and token throughput against the GenAI gateway, the signature of model-extraction and data exfiltration via the inference API. Aggregates calls, summed tokens, and distinct models touched per API key / identity / source IP. Triage threshold: investigate callers with token throughput far above their baseline. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024 (Exfiltration via ML Inference API).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: LLM Jailbreak Detected (AML.T0054)
rules.description: Detects jailbreak attempts at the GenAI/LLM inference gateway. Fires when the guardrail classifies a request as a jailbreak or sets the jailbreak flag (DAN, developer mode, unrestricted roleplay), ranking caller identity, API key, source IP, and model. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0054 (LLM Jailbreak).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: ML Supply-Chain / Model Provenance Failure (AML.T0010)
rules.description: Detects model loads where provenance validation failed at the GenAI gateway: an invalid/missing model signature or a load from an untrusted source. Fires on the provenance guardrail category or an invalid signature, ranking the model, source, and host. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0010 (ML Supply Chain Compromise).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: Full Model Access / Weight Export (AML.T0044)
rules.description: Detects attempts to exfiltrate model artifacts (weights/checkpoints) through the GenAI gateway admin/export surface, the signature of model theft / full model access. Fires on model export operations or export/weights/download routes, ranking caller identity, API key, and source IP. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0044 (Full ML Model Access) / model theft.

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: LLM Prompt Injection Detected (AML.T0051)
rules.description: Detects prompt-injection attempts at the GenAI/LLM inference gateway. Fires when the gateway guardrail classifies a request as prompt injection or sets the injection flag, ranking the offending caller identity, API key, source IP, and targeted model. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051 (LLM Prompt Injection).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: LLM Sensitive-Information Disclosure (AML.T0057)
rules.description: Detects attempts to coerce the LLM into disclosing system prompts, secrets, or sensitive context at the GenAI gateway. Fires when the guardrail flags sensitive disclosure or the data-leak flag is set (often paired with a REDACT verdict), ranking caller identity, API key, and source IP. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0057 (LLM Data Leakage / sensitive information disclosure).

rules.title: GenAI Gateway: Unauthorized / Rejected Inference Access (AML.T0040)
rules.description: Detects unauthorized or rejected access to the GenAI inference gateway. Filters auth/authorization failures (401, 403) and ranks the offending identity, API key, and source IP. Repeated rejections from one identity or a revoked key indicate credential probing or unauthorized model use. MITRE ATLAS AML.T0040 (ML Inference API Access).

rules.title: gMSA Managed Password Read Attempt
rules.description: Detects attempts to read the msDS-ManagedPassword blob of a group Managed Service Account (gMSA), allowing computation of the account's NTLM hash. Tools include GMSAPasswordReader and Get-ADServiceAccount -Properties msDS-ManagedPassword. GOAD exposes gmsaDragon as a gMSA read target.

rules.title: Golden Ticket: RC4 Encrypted TGT Request
rules.description: Detects Kerberos TGT requests (4768) or renewals (4770) using RC4 encryption. Golden tickets forged with krbtgt hash use RC4; modern AD defaults to AES256.

rules.title: GPO Abuse via SharpGPOAbuse
rules.description: Detects SharpGPOAbuse, which weaponizes write access to a Group Policy Object to add an immediate scheduled task, a local admin, or a logon/startup script that executes on all linked computers. GOAD includes GPO write ACL paths.

rules.title: GPO Scheduled Task Written to SYSVOL
rules.description: Detects writes of a ScheduledTasks.xml (or GPT.ini bump) under the SYSVOL Policies path, the on-disk artifact left by GPO-abuse tools that push an immediate scheduled task to every machine the GPO applies to.

rules.title: Group Policy Preferences Password Extraction
rules.description: Detects tools searching for cpassword in Group Policy Preferences XML files.

rules.title: Hidden PowerShell Window Execution
rules.description: Detects PowerShell execution with hidden window style, commonly used to avoid user visibility.

rules.title: Image File Execution Options Debugger
rules.description: Detects setting of Image File Execution Options debugger for persistence or tampering.

rules.title: Impacket atexec Remote Scheduled Task Execution
rules.description: Detects impacket atexec, which creates a transient scheduled task on a remote host (via the ATSvc/Task Scheduler) to run a command and redirect output to an admin share. Indicators: a task launched by the Task Scheduler running cmd with output redirected to ADMIN$/a temp marker file.

rules.title: Impacket wmiexec Remote Command Execution
rules.description: Detects impacket wmiexec-style remote execution, where commands run via WMI (Win32_Process Create) with output redirected to a local admin share, e.g. 'cmd.exe /Q /c <cmd> 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<ts> 2>&1'. A classic semi-interactive lateral-movement pattern.

rules.title: Indirect Command Execution via Forfiles
rules.description: Detects usage of forfiles.exe for indirect command execution to bypass application controls.

rules.title: Insecure Deserialization Attack Detected
rules.description: Detects insecure deserialization attacks where malicious serialized objects are sent to achieve remote code execution. OWASP A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures.

rules.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference Detected
rules.description: Detects IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) attacks where users access resources belonging to other users by manipulating object IDs in URLs. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: InstallUtil Application Whitelisting Bypass
rules.description: Detects usage of InstallUtil.exe to execute code through .NET assemblies, bypassing application controls.

rules.title: Internal Monologue NTLM Hash Theft
rules.description: Detects Internal Monologue attack which retrieves NTLM hashes without touching LSASS.

rules.title: JavaScript Execution via Node.js
rules.description: Detects Node.js executing inline JavaScript or scripts from suspicious locations.

rules.title: Kerberoasting: RC4 Encrypted Service Ticket Request
rules.description: Detects Kerberos service ticket requests (Event 4769) using RC4 encryption (TicketEncryptionType 0x17) which is a strong indicator of Kerberoasting. Modern AD environments use AES256 (0x12) by default; RC4 requests are anomalous and typically indicate tools like Rubeus, Invoke-Kerberoast, or GetUserSPNs.py extracting service ticket hashes for offline cracking.

rules.title: Kerberoasting: SPN Sweep - Multiple Service Tickets from Single Account
rules.description: Detects a single account requesting service tickets for multiple distinct services in a short window, indicating SPN sweep for offline hash cracking. Rubeus kerberoast and Invoke-Kerberoast request tickets for all SPN-enabled accounts rapidly. Normal users request 1-3 service tickets; attackers request 10+.

rules.title: Kerberos Ticket Export via Mimikatz
rules.description: Detects Kerberos ticket exporting commands used in pass-the-ticket and golden ticket attacks.

rules.title: Keylogging via PowerShell Get-Keystrokes
rules.description: Detects PowerShell-based keylogging techniques using API hooks or input monitoring.

rules.title: LAPS Clear-Text Local Admin Password Read Attempt
rules.description: Detects attempts to read the LAPS-managed local administrator password from the ms-Mcs-AdmPwd attribute. Tooling includes LAPSToolkit, Get-AdmPwdPassword, and pyLAPS. GOAD includes a LAPS reader path to recover local admin passwords.

rules.title: Lateral Movement: Account Authenticating from Multiple Sources
rules.description: Detects a single account performing network logons (LogonType 3) from 3+ distinct source IPs — indicates lateral movement sweep or compromised credential reuse.

rules.title: Lateral Tool Transfer via Robocopy
rules.description: Detects usage of robocopy to transfer tools or files to remote systems.

rules.title: LaZagne Credential Harvester
rules.description: Detects execution of LaZagne, an open-source credential harvesting tool that extracts passwords from multiple applications.

rules.title: Linux Archive Data Collected for Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects creation of compressed archives from sensitive directories. Attackers archive collected data (credentials, source code, configs) before exfiltrating to reduce transfer size and avoid detection.

rules.title: Linux At Job Scheduled
rules.description: Detects scheduling of at jobs for deferred command execution. Attackers use at jobs for timed persistence or delayed payload execution.

rules.title: Linux Bind Shell Listener
rules.description: Detects creation of bind shell listeners using netcat, socat, or ncat. Bind shells open a listening port that allows remote attackers to connect and execute commands.

rules.title: Linux Boopkit eBPF Rootkit Activity
rules.description: Detects boopkit/eBPF rootkit activity including BPF program/map loading, XDP attachment, and magic-packet triggered command execution. Boopkit hides reverse shells via eBPF and is invoked by attacker-crafted SYN packets.

rules.title: Linux Container Escape Attempt
rules.description: Detects patterns indicating container escape attempts including access to Docker socket, cgroup manipulation, or nsenter usage from within containers.

rules.title: Linux Crontab Modification
rules.description: Detects crontab command execution, which may indicate persistence via scheduled tasks.

rules.title: Linux Cryptominer Activity Detected
rules.description: Detects execution of known cryptomining software or command patterns associated with unauthorized cryptocurrency mining on compromised hosts.

rules.title: Linux DNS Tunneling Detected
rules.description: Detects execution of DNS tunneling tools such as iodine, dns2tcp, or dnscat2 used to exfiltrate data or establish covert C2 channels over DNS.

rules.title: Linux Encrypted Channel C2 Communication
rules.description: Detects establishment of encrypted communication channels that may indicate C2 activity. Attackers use OpenSSL, stunnel, and encrypted reverse shells to establish covert command and control over TLS/SSL.

rules.title: Linux Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
rules.description: Detects data exfiltration using non-standard protocols. Attackers use tools like netcat, socat, or raw sockets to send data over TCP/UDP to external hosts, bypassing HTTP/HTTPS-based monitoring.

rules.title: Linux External Remote Service Abuse
rules.description: Detects suspicious use of external remote services (SSH, VPN, RDP via xfreerdp) from unusual or unauthorized sources. Attackers abuse external-facing remote services with stolen or default credentials to gain initial access.

rules.title: Linux Hidden File or Directory Creation in Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects creation of hidden files or directories (starting with dot) in world-writable or suspicious locations commonly used by attackers to stash tools and payloads.

rules.title: Linux History File Cleared
rules.description: Detects clearing of command history files or unsetting of history environment variable, a common anti-forensics technique.

rules.title: Linux Hosts File Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of /etc/hosts or /etc/resolv.conf. Attackers modify these to redirect traffic, bypass security controls, or perform DNS hijacking.

rules.title: Linux Kernel Module Loaded from Temp Directory
rules.description: Detects loading of kernel modules from temporary directories (/tmp, /dev/shm, /var/tmp), a strong indicator of rootkit installation.

rules.title: Linux LD_PRELOAD Library Hijacking
rules.description: Detects LD_PRELOAD environment variable injection or /etc/ld.so.preload modification, used for library hijacking and process injection.

rules.title: Linux Log File Tampering
rules.description: Detects deletion, truncation, or clearing of system log files. Attackers tamper with logs to cover their tracks and hinder incident response.

rules.title: Linux Network Service Scanning
rules.description: Detects network scanning tools (nmap, masscan, zmap) used for host and service discovery. Attackers use these tools during the discovery phase to map network topology and identify vulnerable services.

rules.title: Linux Password File Direct Modification
rules.description: Detects direct writes to /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow files, bypassing normal user management tools. This could indicate account creation for persistence.

rules.title: Linux Post-Exploitation Enumeration Script
rules.description: Detects execution of common Linux post-exploitation enumeration scripts such as LinPEAS, LinEnum, or linux-exploit-suggester used during privilege escalation.

rules.title: Linux Process Execution from /dev/shm
rules.description: Detects execution of processes from /dev/shm (shared memory filesystem). This in-memory filesystem is frequently used by attackers to stage and execute payloads without touching disk.

rules.title: Linux Process Injection via Ptrace
rules.description: Detects use of ptrace for process injection or debugging, which can be used to inject malicious code into running processes.

rules.title: Linux Process Memory Access via /proc
rules.description: Detects reading process memory via /proc filesystem entries (maps, mem, environ). Used to extract credentials, secrets, or encryption keys from running processes.

rules.title: Linux Proxy and Tunneling Tool Detected
rules.description: Detects proxy and tunneling tools used to create covert communication channels. Attackers use tools like chisel, ligolo, reGeorg, and frp to proxy C2 traffic through compromised hosts and evade network security controls.

rules.title: Linux Reverse Shell Detected
rules.description: Detects common reverse shell patterns in process execution, indicating potential remote access backdoor activity.

rules.title: Linux Sensitive Data Collection from Local System
rules.description: Detects mass access to sensitive system files. Attackers collect data from local systems including SSH keys, cloud credentials, database configs, and application secrets for further exploitation or exfiltration.

rules.title: Linux Setuid Binary Creation
rules.description: Detects creation of setuid/setgid binaries. Attackers set the SUID bit on binaries to maintain root-level access even when running as unprivileged users. This is a classic privilege escalation persistence technique.

rules.title: Linux Shell Profile Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of shell profile files (.bashrc, .bash_profile, .profile) for persistence. Malicious commands added to these files execute on every user login.

rules.title: Linux SSH Authorized Keys Modified
rules.description: Detects modifications to SSH authorized_keys files, which may indicate backdoor access being established.

rules.title: Linux SSH Failed Login
rules.description: Detects failed SSH login attempts.

rules.title: Linux SSH Tunneling Detected
rules.description: Detects SSH tunneling and port forwarding commands used for pivoting, data exfiltration, or bypassing network security controls.

rules.title: Linux Sudo Usage
rules.description: Detects execution of sudo commands.

rules.title: Linux Sudoers File Modification
rules.description: Detects modifications to the sudoers file or files in sudoers.d directory. Attackers modify sudoers to escalate privileges or maintain persistent elevated access.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Cron Job Content
rules.description: Detects cron job entries containing suspicious patterns such as downloading payloads, piping to shell, or encoding commands typically used for persistence.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Download to /tmp
rules.description: Detects file downloads to /tmp directory using common tools. Attackers frequently stage payloads in /tmp as it is world-writable.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Network Traffic Redirect
rules.description: Detects iptables or nftables modifications that redirect network traffic, which could indicate man-in-the-middle attacks or traffic interception.

rules.title: Linux System Owner and User Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands used to discover system owner, logged-in users, and user context. Attackers run these commands early in post-compromise to understand their privilege level and identify other active users.

rules.title: Linux Systemd Service Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation or enabling of systemd services, a common persistence mechanism where attackers install malicious services that survive reboots.

rules.title: Linux Web Shell File Creation
rules.description: Detects file creation or modification in common web directories that may indicate web shell deployment for persistent remote access.

rules.title: Local Group Membership Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands enumerating local group membership, especially administrators.

rules.title: LSA Secrets Registry Extraction
rules.description: Detects extraction of LSA Secrets from the registry, which stores service account passwords and other sensitive data.

rules.title: LSASS Clone via ProcDump Evasion
rules.description: Detects LSASS cloning techniques that create a clone of the LSASS process to dump credentials without directly accessing the original.

rules.title: LSASS Memory Dump via Comsvcs DLL
rules.description: Detects LSASS memory dumping using comsvcs.dll MiniDump export, a technique to extract credentials without dropping tools.

rules.title: Masquerading System Binary in Non-Standard Path
rules.description: Detects execution of common system binaries from non-standard locations, indicating masquerading.

rules.title: Mass Assignment Attack Detected
rules.description: Detects mass assignment attacks where extra parameters are injected into API requests to modify protected fields (isAdmin, role, price). OWASP A04:2021 Insecure Design.

rules.title: Mimikatz: Command and Module Indicators in Process Logs
rules.description: Detects Mimikatz command and module strings in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation and 4624/4648 logon events. Covers sekurlsa, lsadump, kerberos, dpapi, crypto modules and common Mimikatz post-exploitation patterns. Works with real Windows Security Events via Original Log Content matching.

rules.title: Mimikatz: Command and Module Indicators in Process Logs
rules.description: Detects Mimikatz command and module strings in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation and 4624/4648 logon events. Covers sekurlsa, lsadump, kerberos, dpapi, crypto modules and common Mimikatz post-exploitation patterns. Works with real Windows Security Events via Original Log Content matching.

rules.title: MSBuild Execution from Non-Standard Location
rules.description: Detects MSBuild.exe executing project files from user-writable directories.

rules.title: MSHTA JavaScript Execution
rules.description: Detects MSHTA executing inline JavaScript or VBScript content.

rules.title: Named Pipe Impersonation via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects named pipe impersonation technique for privilege escalation via PowerShell.

rules.title: Netsh Helper DLL Persistence
rules.description: Detects registration of netsh helper DLLs which execute every time netsh.exe is invoked.

rules.title: Network Share Enumeration via Net View
rules.description: Detects network share enumeration using net view command.

rules.title: New Local Account Created via Net.exe
rules.description: Detects creation of local accounts using net.exe or net1.exe.

rules.title: NPPSpy Credential Interception
rules.description: Detects installation of NPPSpy or similar credential-intercepting DLLs in the Network Provider order.

rules.title: NTDS.dit Database Copy Attempt
rules.description: Detects attempts to copy the NTDS.dit Active Directory database file using ntdsutil, vssadmin, or esentutl.

rules.title: ntlmrelayx NTLM Relay Execution Indicators
rules.description: Detects execution of impacket ntlmrelayx, used to relay coerced NTLM authentication to LDAP/SMB/HTTP targets for RBCD, ADCS ESC8, or remote code execution. GOAD's NTLM relay scenario relays a coerced hash to a target host.

rules.title: OCI Action: AddUserToGroup
rules.description: Detects the execution of AddUserToGroup action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: AttachInternetGateway
rules.description: Detects the execution of AttachInternetGateway action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: CreateBucket
rules.description: Detects the execution of CreateBucket action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: CreateGroup
rules.description: Detects the execution of CreateGroup action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: CreateInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of CreateInstance action in OCI.

rules.description: Detects the execution of CreateInternetGateway action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: CreateKey
