hunting_queries.description: Histogram of scenario hits per hour across the last 7 days. Spot bursts of activity and pivot timing between scenarios.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Critical Alerts (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Tile count of CAT I / critical hunt scenarios observed. All 15 scenarios in the oci-coordinator showcase carry critical severity, so this equals the total scenario hits.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Log Source → Process (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Sunburst rollup of scenario hits grouped by Log Source then Process Name. Compact attacker-toolchain view without eval/case (parser-native fields only) — useful for spotting which process families drive each log-source's noise.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Distinct Processes Observed (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Distinct count of process names observed across the 15 oci-coordinator hunting scenarios. Higher cardinality signals broader attacker toolchain breadth across the kill-chain.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Log Source Breakdown (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Bar chart of scenario hits grouped by Log Source. Identifies which telemetry stream (Linux audit, APM, Web, OCI Audit) is generating the most threat-hunt noise so analysts pivot to the right log first.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Top Affected Hosts (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Horizontal bar chart of hosts ranked by number of scenario hits across the 15 oci-coordinator hunts. Spot stations under sustained attack or pivot points.

hunting_queries.title: Coordinator Scenarios: Total Hits (l7d)
hunting_queries.description: Total number of events matching any of the 15 oci-coordinator hunting scenarios over the last 7 days. Combines Linux audit/syslog, OCI Audit, APM application, and web/WAF sources.

hunting_queries.title: CrashFix: Python RAT Activity
hunting_queries.description: Detects CrashFix-style ClickFix variants where browser social engineering leads to Python RAT execution, package retrieval, and HTTPS callback activity.

references.name: Microsoft: ClickFix variant CrashFix deploying Python RAT trojan

hunting_queries.title: Hunting: Credential Attack Correlation (PowerShell + Mimikatz + Kerberoast)
hunting_queries.description: Correlates credential attack indicators across Windows Security Events. The content filter matches PowerShell download cradles, Mimikatz/LSASS dumping, and Kerberoasting (RC4 ticket) activity; events are classified by Event ID into Kerberoasting (4769) vs Endpoint_Credential_Tooling (4688), and entities exhibiting both stages are surfaced. (OCI LA eval does not support 'like' for inline content classification, so staging is Event-ID based.)

hunting_queries.title: Attack Activity Timeline by Log Source (l21d)
hunting_queries.description: Cross-domain attack-volume timeline over the 21-day demo window, bucketed hourly and split by Log Source so analysts can see when each attack story (cloud audit, Linux, Windows, WAF, network) lit up and drill into the spike.

hunting_queries.title: Cloud: Critical OCI Audit Events (l21d)
hunting_queries.description: Tile count of destructive or high-risk OCI control-plane actions (delete/terminate/remove) across the audit log. Headline KPI for the Cloud/Identity attack story on the SOC command center.

hunting_queries.title: Top Attacker Source IPs - OCI Audit (l21d)
hunting_queries.description: Ranks external source IPs by destructive/anomalous OCI control-plane activity, with the count of distinct event types each IP touched. Click a row to pivot into Log Explorer scoped to that Source IP for the full identity-attack drilldown.

hunting_queries.title: DNS Exfiltration Detection (Entropy Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects potential DNS tunneling and data exfiltration by identifying DNS queries with unusually long subdomain labels. In the demo dataset, domains longer than 14 characters represent suspicious beaconing and staged exfiltration labels while avoiding the short baseline entries. Uses field analysis combined with frequency thresholds.

hunting_queries.title: 2025-2026: Exfiltration After Initial Access
hunting_queries.description: Finds large outbound transfers and firewall data-theft alerts tied to ClickFix/CrashFix, web-to-cloud, and cloud identity abuse traces.

hunting_queries.title: FLF: BITS Exfiltration Hunt
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for BITS abuse through bitsadmin.exe or Start-BitsTransfer commands that reference external HTTP/HTTPS destinations or suspicious file-transfer behavior.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - BITS As Exfil

hunting_queries.title: FLF: Cloud Service Exfiltration Hunt
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious non-browser processes uploading or connecting to trusted cloud services such as Microsoft Graph, OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, and Slack.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Cloud-Based Services as Exfil

hunting_queries.title: FLF: Credential Stuffing Pattern
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for automated credential stuffing across Windows and application authentication logs, highlighting high-volume attempts against many accounts from the same infrastructure.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Credential Stuffing

hunting_queries.title: FLF: DNS C2 Pattern Analysis
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for DNS command-and-control patterns: long or suspicious DNS query names, dynamic DNS infrastructure, high-risk TLDs, and repeated queries by host.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Name System As C2

hunting_queries.title: FLF: Domain Fronting CDN C2 Hunt
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious C2 candidates where non-browser processes repeatedly connect to CDN or cloud-fronting endpoints over HTTPS.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Fronting as C2

hunting_queries.title: FLF: New User Persistence
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for attacker persistence through account creation. Windows Event ID 4720 is surfaced with host, account, and source context for triage.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - New User Added

hunting_queries.title: FLF: Port Knocking Sequence Drilldown
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired link analysis for port knocking: knocks on 7000, 8000, and 9000 followed by SSH on 22, with failed wrong-order attempts preserved for comparison.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Port Knocking on the Wire

hunting_queries.title: FLF: vsagent HTTP Beaconing
hunting_queries.description: FreeLabFriday AdvancedC2 hunt for vsagent-style HTTP beacons: fixed User-Agent, repeated GET /beacon check-ins, and POST /beacon output submission.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - vsagent HTTP Beaconing

hunting_queries.title: Hunting: GOAD/Apex Caldera Attack Chain (Multi-Stage)
hunting_queries.description:
Correlates Caldera adversary phases (Discovery, Credential Access, Lateral Movement) on the 10 GOAD + Apex AD Windows hosts. Maps the c7_run_caldera_operations.sh adversary profiles to Windows Security Event IDs: failed logons (4625) for Brute Force / network logon sweep, explicit credentials (4648) for Pass-the-Ticket / runas, special privileges (4672) for SeDebug elevation, Kerberos service tickets (4769) for Kerberoasting, directory replication (4662) for DCSync, credential read (5379) for Credential Manager dump.

A host with 3+ distinct event types in the lookback indicates an active multi-stage Caldera operation against that GOAD/Apex VM. Use this widget alongside the per-technique tiles to confirm full kill-chain progression vs isolated detections, and to attribute alerts to specific Caldera operations (Discovery/CredAccess/Lateral) rather than treating each tile in isolation.

hunting_queries.title: Hunting: GOAD/Apex Caldera Sandcat Agent Activity
hunting_queries.description:
Surfaces Caldera sandcat-as-splunkd.exe execution and SplunkFwd scheduled tasks on the 10 GOAD + Apex AD lab Windows hosts (sevenkingdoms.local, north.sevenkingdoms.local, essos.local, apex.local domains). Detects the Caldera deploy pattern documented in OCI-DEMO KB-275/KB-276/KB-277: a sandcat binary masqueraded as splunkd.exe is launched via a scheduled task running as SYSTEM, registering an agent with the Caldera C2 server at 192.168.100.78:8888.

The Caldera deploy pipeline runs: (1) Defender disable via Set-MpPreference, (2) write C:/Users/Public/splunkd.exe (sandcat binary), (3) schtasks /Create /SC ONCE /TN SplunkFwd /TR splunkd.exe /RU SYSTEM, (4) schtasks /Run /TN SplunkFwd. Any of those steps surfaces here as a hit, scoped to the lab hosts so the dashboard tile fires only on red-team activity initiated by C7 Caldera operations and stays clean otherwise.

hunting_queries.title: Hunting: Kerberoasting Anomaly - RC4 vs AES Encryption Ratio
hunting_queries.description: Analyzes the ratio of RC4 (0x17) to AES256 (0x12) Kerberos service ticket requests per account. Accounts with >20% RC4 usage are anomalous in modern AD environments. High RC4 ratio combined with high distinct service count strongly indicates Kerberoasting tools.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Data Staging and Exfiltration Indicators
hunting_queries.description: Detects data staging and exfiltration preparation on Linux hosts by identifying hosts where archiving tools (tar, zip, gzip) and network transfer tools (curl, wget, scp, nc) are used in close proximity. Multiple data handling commands on the same host suggest an attacker collecting and exfiltrating data.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Multi-Stage Attack Indicators (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Detects hosts exhibiting multiple attack stage indicators: initial access (SSH failures), persistence (authorized_keys, crontab), execution (reverse shells, downloads). When 3+ different indicator types appear on the same host, it suggests an active multi-stage attack. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

hunting_queries.title: Linux Unusual Outbound Connection Frequency
hunting_queries.description: Detects hosts making an unusually high number of outbound connections, which may indicate C2 beaconing, data exfiltration, or scanning activity.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Persistence Indicator Score (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Calculates a persistence risk score per host by counting distinct persistence mechanism indicators (crontab, systemd, authorized_keys, shell profiles, at-jobs, sudoers). A host with multiple persistence vectors indicates thorough attacker entrenchment. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

hunting_queries.title: Linux Process Execution from Unusual Paths
hunting_queries.description: Identifies processes running from world-writable or temporary directories, which is a common indicator of post-exploitation activity.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Rare Process Detection (Stacking)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Linux endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 100 times across the active search window, which keeps the demo resilient for a 14-day dataset and repeated reingestion while still surfacing uncommon tooling.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Rare Process Execution by Host
hunting_queries.description: Surfaces processes that have only been seen on a small number of hosts, which may indicate attacker tools, backdoors, or lateral movement payloads.

hunting_queries.title: Linux User Privilege Change Correlation
hunting_queries.description: Correlates sudo/su usage with subsequent sensitive file access or network activity, revealing post-escalation attacker behavior.

hunting_queries.title: Hunting: Logon Anomaly - Account Activity Profiling
hunting_queries.description: Profiles user account activity across Windows Security Events using the parsed 'Target User Name' field: total logons, unique hosts authenticated to, explicit credential delegations (4648), privilege assignments (4672), and failed-logon ratio (4625). Surfaces accounts with many hosts, high delegation, or high failure rate.

hunting_queries.title: MELTS: Attack Path Link Drilldown
hunting_queries.description: Link analysis for the 2025-2026 campaign. Groups trace IDs to log sources so analysts can pivot from entry point to host execution, cloud abuse, C2, and exfiltration evidence.

hunting_queries.title: MELTS: 2025-2026 Attack Signal Overview
hunting_queries.description: Correlates Metrics-style counts, Events, Logs, Traces, and Security state for the modern 2025-2026 demo attacks: ClickFix/CrashFix, SharePoint ToolShell, RMM abuse, AiTM token replay, and staged exfiltration.

references.name: MELT observability pattern: Metrics, Events, Logs, and Traces

hunting_queries.title: MELTS: 2025-2026 Attack Timeline
hunting_queries.description: Time-series view of the modern attack scenarios across endpoint, web, application, network, firewall, and cloud-control-plane logs. Use this as the first pivot before drilling into attack-specific widgets.

hunting_queries.title: Geographic Health: Cloud Provider Summary
hunting_queries.description: Summarizes instance health by cloud provider (OCI, Azure, AWS, GCP) showing total instances, healthy/degraded/unreachable counts, and provider-level fleet distribution.

hunting_queries.title: Geographic Health: Instance Detail with Coordinates
hunting_queries.description: Shows individual instance health status with geographic coordinates, cloud provider, region, role, and key metrics. Each row represents the latest heartbeat from an instance — suitable for map pin placement.

hunting_queries.title: Geographic Health: Regional Status on Global Map
hunting_queries.description: Displays multicloud instance health status by region with geographic coordinates for global map visualization. Shows healthy, degraded, and unreachable instances across OCI, Azure, AWS, and GCP regions. Use with the Map visualization type in OCI Log Analytics.

hunting_queries.title: Geographic Health: Service Tier Status
hunting_queries.description: Shows health status grouped by service tier (frontend, application, database, cache, compute, orchestration, observability) across all cloud providers and regions.

hunting_queries.title: Geographic Health: Unhealthy Regions Alert
hunting_queries.description: Identifies regions with degraded or unreachable instances across all cloud providers. Highlights regions needing immediate attention for operational response.

hunting_queries.title: C2 Beaconing Detection (Periodic Connection Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects command-and-control beaconing by identifying hosts making periodic outbound connections to the same destination. C2 implants typically beacon at regular intervals. Groups network connections by source-destination pair and counts occurrences — a high count to a single destination suggests automated beaconing rather than human browsing.

hunting_queries.title: OCI After-Hours IAM Activity (Time-Based Anomaly)
hunting_queries.description: Detects IAM configuration changes made outside normal business hours (before 06:00 or after 22:00 UTC). Identity changes at unusual hours may indicate a compromised account being used when legitimate users are not monitoring. Uses time-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection - temporal patterns).

hunting_queries.title: OCI API Call Burst by User
hunting_queries.description: Detects users making an unusually high number of API calls in a short window, which may indicate automated enumeration or data exfiltration scripts.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Console Login Brute Force (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects brute force or credential stuffing against OCI Console by counting failed login attempts per user. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to identify accounts with more than 3 failed attempts in the time window.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Cross-Compartment Activity Anomaly
hunting_queries.description: Identifies users operating across an unusual number of compartments, which may indicate lateral movement or reconnaissance after initial access.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Failed Action Spike by Source IP
hunting_queries.description: Identifies source IPs with a high ratio of failed to successful API calls, which may indicate credential stuffing or permission enumeration.

hunting_queries.title: OCI IAM and Fusion Activity Correlation
hunting_queries.description: Correlates OCI IAM identity events with Fusion Apps sign-in/sign-out and ESS audit activity. Flags identities or source IPs seen in both OCI and Fusion during the search window, which may indicate account misuse or suspicious cross-platform identity activity.

hunting_queries.title: OCI IAM Rapid Configuration Changes (Anomaly Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Detects users making a high volume of IAM changes in a short time window. A burst of identity changes (policy updates, user creation, group modifications) from a single principal may indicate account compromise or insider threat. Uses frequency analysis with threshold (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Multiple Users from Same IP (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Detects a single IP address being used to authenticate as multiple different OCI users. This pattern may indicate credential stuffing, shared compromised infrastructure, or an attacker testing multiple stolen credentials from the same origin. Uses distinct count grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Off-Hours Administrative Activity
hunting_queries.description: Surfaces administrative API calls occurring outside business hours (evenings, weekends), which may indicate unauthorized access or compromised accounts.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Privilege Escalation Chain Detection
hunting_queries.description: Detects potential privilege escalation by identifying users who perform a sequence of IAM operations: creating policies, groups, or dynamic groups followed by adding themselves to privileged groups. Combines multiple identity control plane events from the same principal to surface escalation chains.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Resource Deletion Wave
hunting_queries.description: Detects mass resource deletion patterns where multiple resources are deleted in rapid succession, indicating potential ransomware, sabotage, or account compromise.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Resource Destruction Spike (Anomaly Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies principals performing an abnormally high number of destructive operations (delete, terminate). A spike in resource destruction from a single user may indicate a compromised account performing sabotage, ransomware-style destruction, or an insider threat. Uses threshold-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Unusual API Caller - First Seen User-Agent
hunting_queries.description: Identifies API calls from user-agents that have not been seen in the baseline period, which may indicate new tooling or unauthorized API access.

hunting_queries.title: Octo Chaos — Stale Scenario State
hunting_queries.description: Identifies chaos-audit events where a scenario remains active past its TTL. Used by the chaos-cleanup playbook (tier: low) to auto-clear the registry.

hunting_queries.title: Octo Checkout — DB Slowness Correlated With Chaos
hunting_queries.description: Surfaces p95 DB latency spikes on the Octo checkout workflow and splits them by whether a chaos scenario was active. Used by the coordinator db-slow-remediation playbook.

hunting_queries.title: Octo WAF Detections vs App 5xx
hunting_queries.description: Joins OCI WAF detection events with Shop/CRM 5xx responses using the shared X-Request-Id header. Used to build the Security Posture dashboard and to feed the waf-tighten-suggest playbook.

hunting_queries.title: RMM: Post-Compromise Remote Access Activity
hunting_queries.description: Detects remote monitoring and management tooling used after compromise, including ScreenConnect, AnyDesk, and Atera process execution plus outbound relay traffic.

references.name: CISA: Remote Monitoring and Management software security guidance

hunting_queries.title: SharePoint ToolShell: Exploitation Attempts
hunting_queries.description: Detects SharePoint ToolShell-style exploitation by looking for ToolPane and spinstall0.aspx activity across WAF, load balancer, web application, and application trace telemetry.

references.name: Microsoft MSRC: Customer guidance for SharePoint vulnerability CVE-2025-53770

hunting_queries.title: SharePoint ToolShell: Webshell Post-Exploit
hunting_queries.description: Finds post-exploitation command attempts against SharePoint webshell paths, especially spinstall0.aspx command execution, backend 500s, and high-severity application spans.

references.name: Microsoft MSRC: SharePoint CVE-2025-53770 customer guidance

hunting_queries.title: SSH Brute Force Detection (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies SSH brute force attacks by counting failed login attempts per source IP. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface IPs with more than 5 failed attempts, indicating automated credential guessing.

hunting_queries.title: Login Activity Time-Series Anomaly
hunting_queries.description: Detects anomalous spikes in authentication activity using time-bucketed analysis. Groups login events into hourly buckets and flags hours with significantly more activity than the baseline. Uses timecluster-style analysis to identify burst authentication patterns that may indicate credential stuffing or compromised account usage.

hunting_queries.title: WAF Attack Frequency by Source IP (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies source IPs generating the most WAF block events. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking) to surface prolific attackers targeting the web application. IPs with high block counts indicate automated attack tools.

hunting_queries.title: WAF Multi-Attack Vector Scoring (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Calculates a threat score per source IP based on the diversity of attack types (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, command injection, SSRF). An IP triggering multiple different CRS rule categories indicates a sophisticated attacker using multiple techniques. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods).

hunting_queries.title: SQL Injection Pattern Stacking (Rare Value Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Stacks unique SQL injection payloads observed in WAF events to identify attack tool signatures and novel injection techniques. Rare payloads may indicate custom exploits or new attack vectors. Uses rare value stacking (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking).

hunting_queries.title: Web Attack Geographic Anomaly (Rare Country Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies attack traffic originating from unusual countries by stacking WAF events by country code. Rare countries with high attack volumes may indicate targeted campaigns from specific threat actor regions.

hunting_queries.title: Web Application Brute Force Detection (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies IPs performing brute force attacks against web login endpoints by counting 401/403 responses. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking) to surface credential stuffing and brute force campaigns.

hunting_queries.title: Web Directory Scanning IP Clustering (Anomaly Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Clusters IPs performing directory enumeration by counting distinct 404-generating paths. A high number of unique 404 paths from a single IP indicates systematic directory/file scanning using tools like dirb, gobuster, or ffuf.

hunting_queries.title: OWASP Multi-Stage Web Attack Chain (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Correlates web attack indicators across WAF, Load Balancer, and application logs to identify multi-stage attack chains. An IP that triggers WAF blocks, generates auth failures, and accesses sensitive endpoints is likely executing a coordinated attack campaign. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

hunting_queries.title: Web Scanner Tool Identification (User Agent Stacking)
hunting_queries.description: Stacks user agents across all web traffic to identify automated scanning tools, attack frameworks, and bot traffic. Non-browser user agents generating high request volumes from a small set of source IPs indicate active reconnaissance or automated exploitation.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Attack Path Link Analysis
hunting_queries.description: Link analysis groups all events carrying the web-to-cloud Trace ID or C2/object-storage pivots into a single path. Use this widget to drill from WAF entry to app host, network egress, firewall alert, and OCI Audit object access.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Correlated Attack Timeline
hunting_queries.description: Summarizes the web-to-cloud attack chain across WAF, load balancer, application, host, VCN Flow, Network Firewall, Sysmon network, and OCI Audit logs. The shared Trace ID and fixed attacker/C2 pivots let analysts follow entry point, credential access, C2, cloud abuse, and exfiltration in one widget.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: OCI Audit Cloud Abuse
hunting_queries.description: Filters OCI Audit for cloud discovery and Object Storage actions associated with the compromised service identity and web-to-cloud Trace ID.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Compromised Cloud Identity
hunting_queries.description: Highlights OCI Audit activity performed by the compromised service identity after instance metadata access. The widget surfaces Object Storage enumeration, object reads, and pre-authenticated request creation.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Compromised Machines
hunting_queries.description: Shows hosts and private IPs tied to the compromise, including application host activity, Windows C2 telemetry, VCN flows, and firewall sessions. Analysts can pivot from host names to network tuples and command lines.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Entry Point and SSRF Evidence
hunting_queries.description: Identifies the initial web request that reached the application tier and attempted access to the cloud instance metadata endpoint. Use the Request URL, Client IP, backend host, and Trace ID to start the investigation.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Exfiltrated Data Evidence
hunting_queries.description: Combines application export messages, VCN Flow egress, Network Firewall threat alerts, and OCI Audit object reads to show what data left the environment and which destination received it.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: Network Firewall C2 and Threat Alerts
hunting_queries.description: Correlates Network Firewall traffic and threat records for the C2 destination. Threat Name, Firewall Rule, byte counts, and action values show whether the exfiltration was alerted, allowed, or denied.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: MITRE Stage Breakdown
hunting_queries.description: Sunburst-friendly rollup of the web-to-cloud chain by evidence source and threat label. It shows whether evidence is present across initial access, host, network, firewall, and cloud-control-plane telemetry.

hunting_queries.title: Web-to-Cloud: VCN Egress and Exfil Flows
hunting_queries.description: Surfaces accepted VCN Flow Log records from the compromised application host to the C2/exfil destination and OCI service endpoints. Large Bytes Sent values provide the primary network indicator for data loss.

hunting_queries.title: Windows High-Entropy DNS Queries
hunting_queries.description: Surfaces DNS queries with unusually long or high-entropy domain names, which are characteristic of DNS tunneling, DGA malware, or data exfiltration over DNS.

hunting_queries.title: Windows Lateral Movement Timeline
hunting_queries.description: Correlates SMB connections, remote service creation, and WinRM sessions to build a timeline of lateral movement activity across the environment.

hunting_queries.title: Windows LOLBin Usage Frequency Anomaly
hunting_queries.description: Identifies hosts with abnormally high usage of living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBins), which may indicate fileless malware or attacker tool usage.

hunting_queries.title: Windows Rare Parent-Child Process Relationships
hunting_queries.description: Identifies unusual parent-child process pairs that deviate from normal system behavior. Rare combinations may indicate process injection, living-off-the-land, or malware execution.

hunting_queries.title: Windows Credential Access Tool Cluster (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Groups credential access tool indicators by host and user to identify active credential harvesting campaigns. When multiple credential dumping techniques (Mimikatz, LSASS access, Kerberoasting, NTDS.dit) appear on the same host, it indicates systematic credential theft. Uses grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Defense Evasion Score (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Calculates a defense evasion risk score per host by counting distinct evasion indicators: event log clearing, AMSI bypass, firewall modification, UAC bypass, process hollowing. Hosts exhibiting multiple evasion techniques indicate sophisticated attackers actively disabling security controls. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Lateral Movement Tool Cluster (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Groups lateral movement tool usage by host to identify systems being used as pivot points. When multiple lateral movement tools (PsExec, RDP, WinRM, remote shares) appear on the same host, it strongly suggests active lateral movement. Uses grouping/clustering (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Suspiciously Long Command Line (Field Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Flags processes with unusually long command lines (>500 chars). Attackers frequently use long command lines for encoded payloads, obfuscated scripts, and base64-encoded commands. Uses eval-based field creation (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Searching/Filtering method with field creation).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Process from Unusual Path (Rare Value Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies processes executing from non-standard paths commonly used by attackers (Temp, Public, AppData, Downloads). Legitimate software typically runs from Program Files or System32. Uses rare path analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking + Field Analysis).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Rare Process Detection (Stacking)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Windows endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 80 times across the active search window, which keeps the demo resilient for a 14-day dataset and repeated reingestion while still highlighting uncommon attacker tooling.
